21_6_2.ESV: a man to whom God gives wealth, possessions, and honor, so that he lacks nothing of all that he desires, yet God does not give him power to enjoy them, but a stranger enjoys them. This is vanity;  it is a grievous evil.
21_6_2.NASB: a man to whom God has given riches and wealth and honor so that his soul lacks nothing of all that he desires; yet God has not empowered him to eat from them, for a foreigner enjoys them. This is vanity and a severe affliction.

This is vanity and it is a [cause of] great distress.
21_6_2.MSG: There are people, for instance, on whom God showers everything—money, property, reputation—all they ever wanted or dreamed of. And then God doesn't let them enjoy it. Some stranger comes along and has all the fun. It's more of what I'm calling smoke. A bad business.

21_10_2.NLT:
A wise person chooses the right road;

21_10_2.WEB: A wise man’s heart is at his right hand, but a fool’s heart at his left.

21_10_2.NRSVCE:

21_10_3.NKJV: Even when a fool walks along the way, He lacks wisdom, And he shows everyone that he is a fool.
21_10_3.KJV: Yea also, when he that is a fool<S>5530</S> walketh<S>1980</S> by the way<S>1870</S>, his wisdom<S>3820</S> faileth<S>2638</S> him, and he saith<S>559</S> to every one that he is a fool<S>5530</S>. <sup>his: Heb. his heart</sup>

21_10_3.NLT:

21_10_3.MSG:

21_10_3.NRSVCE:

21_10_4.NKJV: If the spirit of the ruler rises against you,  Do not leave your post; For conciliation pacifies great offenses.

21_10_4.WEB: If the spirit of the ruler rises up against you, don’t leave your place; for gentleness lays great offenses to rest.

21_10_5.NKJV: There is an evil I have seen under the sun, As an error proceeding from the ruler:

21_10_7.NKJV: I have seen servants on horses, While princes walk on the ground like servants.
21_10_7.KJV: I have seen<S>7200</S> servants<S>5650</S> upon horses<S>5483</S>, and princes<S>8269</S> walking<S>1980</S> as servants<S>5650</S> upon the earth<S>776</S>.

21_10_12.NKJV: The words of a wise man’s mouth are gracious, But the lips of a fool shall swallow him up;

21_10_13.NKJV: The words of his mouth begin with foolishness, And the end of his talk is raving madness.

21_10_16.NKJV: Woe to you, O land, when your king is a child, And your princes feast in the morning!

21_10_16.CSB17:

21_10_16.NRSVCE:

21_10_17.KJV: Blessed<S>835</S> art thou, O land<S>776</S>, when thy king<S>4428</S> is the son<S>1121</S> of nobles<S>2715</S>, and thy princes<S>8269</S> eat<S>398</S> in due season<S>6256</S>, for strength<S>1369</S>, and not for drunkenness<S>8358</S>!

and your princes feast at the proper time —

Lucky the land whose king is mature,

21_10_17.NRSVCE:

21_10_18.NKJV: Because of laziness the building decays, And through idleness of hands the house leaks.

21_10_18.NRSVCE:
